title: अग्नि चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अग्नि देव चालीसा ॥

॥ दोहा ॥
अग्नि देव की वन्दना, हवन यज्ञ आधार।
देवताओं के मुख हो, वाहक हव्य अपार॥
सप्त जिह्वा प्रज्वलित, तेजस्वी भगवान।
अग्नि चालीसा पढ़ें, करें कृपा निधान॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय जय अग्नि देव भगवाना।
यज्ञ पुरुष हव्यवाहन नाना॥
सप्त जिह्वा से प्रकट हुआ तेज।
त्रिलोक में व्यापत तुम्हारा ओज॥ १॥

काली कराली मनोजवा,
सुलोहिता धूम्रवर्णा उज्ज्वला।
स्फुलिंगिनी विश्वरूपी जिह्वा,
सप्त नाम तेरी प्रभा विमला॥ २॥

वेदों में तुम प्रथम वन्दित।
ऋग्वेद प्रथम सूक्त तुम्हें समर्पित॥
अग्निमीळे पुरोहितम् गाया।
यज्ञस्य देवमृत्विजम् बताया॥ ३॥

होतार त्वम् अध्वर्यु साथी।
देव-मानव के बीच सम्बन्ध ग्राही॥
हवि अर्पित जो अग्नि में होती।
देवों तक पहुँचाओ ज्योति॥ ४॥

गृहस्थ के घर नित्य जलो तुम।
गार्हपत्य दक्षिण आहवनीय क्रम॥
तीन रूप में अग्नि विराजे।
यज्ञशाला में नित प्रभु साजे॥ ५॥

स्वाहा देवी तुम्हारी पत्नी।
बिना स्वाहा आहुति नहीं सत्नी॥
पावक नाम तुम्हारा पावन।
सब कुछ शुद्ध करो प्रभु सावन॥ ६॥

जठराग्नि रूप सब में बसते।
अन्न पचाओ प्राण धरते॥
वैश्वानर रूप सदा विराजो।
भक्तों की जठर ज्वाला साजो॥ ७॥

दावाग्नि बनो वन शोधक।
बडवाग्नि सागर में रोधक॥
विद्युत रूप गगन में चमको।
सूर्य रूप त्रिभुवन तमको हटो॥ ८॥

विवाह में साक्षी बनो तुम।
सप्तपदी अग्नि फेरे पावन सुम॥
गृहस्थ धर्म के आधार तुम।
पवित्र बन्धन के सार तुम॥ ९॥

यज्ञ हवन पूजा में पूजित।
अग्निहोत्र से लोक सुशोभित॥
नवग्रह शान्ति हवन से होवे।
अग्नि देव की कृपा जो बोवे॥ १०॥

॥ दोहा ॥
अग्नि चालीसा पढ़ें, शुद्धि पावें तन मन।
यज्ञ हवन फलदायी, कृपा करें अग्नि भगवन॥
सप्त जिह्वा वाले प्रभु, हव्यवाहन गुणधाम।
अग्नि देव की कृपा से, पूर्ण हों सब काम॥

॥ इति श्री अग्नि चालीसा सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Agni Deva Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Agni Deva Ki Vandana, Havana Yajna Adhara.
Devataon Ke Mukha Ho, Vahaka Havya Apara.
Sapta Jihva Prajvalita, Tejaswi Bhagavana.
Agni Chalisa Padhen, Karen Kripa Nidhana.

|| Chaupai ||
Jaya Jaya Agni Deva Bhagavana.
Yajna Purusha Havyavahana Nana.
Sapta Jihva Se Prakata Hua Teja.
Triloka Mein Vyapata Tumhara Oja. ||1||

Kali Karali Manojava,
Sulohita Dhumravarna Ujjvala.
Sphulingini Vishvarupi Jihva,
Sapta Nama Teri Prabha Vimala. ||2||

Vedon Mein Tum Prathama Vandita.
Rigveda Prathama Sukta Tumhen Samarpita.
Agnimilhe Purohitam Gaya.
Yajnasya Devam-Ritvijam Bataya. ||3||

Hotara Tvam Adhvaryu Sathi.
Deva-Manava Ke Bicha Sambandha Grahi.
Havi Arpita Jo Agni Mein Hoti.
Devon Taka Pahunchao Jyoti. ||4||

Grihastha Ke Ghara Nitya Jalo Tum.
Garhapatya Dakshina Ahavniya Krama.
Tina Rupa Mein Agni Viraje.
Yajnashala Mein Nit Prabhu Saje. ||5||

Svaha Devi Tumhari Patni.
Bina Svaha Ahuti Nahin Satni.
Pavaka Nama Tumhara Pavana.
Sab Kuchh Shuddha Karo Prabhu Savana. ||6||

Jatharagni Rupa Sab Mein Basate.
Anna Pachao Prana Dharate.
Vaishvanara Rupa Sada Virajo.
Bhakton Ki Jathara Jvala Sajo. ||7||

Davagni Bano Vana Shodhaka.
Badavagni Sagara Mein Rodhaka.
Vidyut Rupa Gagana Mein Chamako.
Surya Rupa Tribhuvana Tamako Hatao. ||8||

Vivaha Mein Sakshi Bano Tum.
Saptapadi Agni Phere Pavana Sum.
Grihastha Dharma Ke Adhara Tum.
Pavitra Bandhana Ke Sara Tum. ||9||

Yajna Havana Puja Mein Pujita.
Agnihotra Se Loka Sushobhita.
Navagraha Shanti Havana Se Hove.
Agni Deva Ki Kripa Jo Bove. ||10||

|| Doha ||
Agni Chalisa Padhen, Shuddhi Paven Tana Mana.
Yajna Havana Phaladayi, Kripa Karen Agni Bhagavana.
Sapta Jihva Vale Prabhu, Havyavahana Gunadhama.
Agni Deva Ki Kripa Se, Purna Hon Saba Kama.

|| Iti Shri Agni Chalisa Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: अग्नि देव की वन्दना — यज्ञ और हवन के आधार, देवताओं के मुख, हव्य (आहुति) के वाहक। सात जिह्वाओं वाले: काली, कराली, मनोजवा, सुलोहिता, धूम्रवर्णा, स्फुलिंगिनी, विश्वरूपी। वेदों में सर्वप्रथम वन्दित — ऋग्वेद का प्रथम सूक्त 'अग्निमीळे पुरोहितम्' इन्हीं को समर्पित। गार्हपत्य, दक्षिण, आहवनीय — तीन रूपों में गृहस्थ के घर विराजते हैं। स्वाहा देवी पत्नी हैं, पावक नाम से सब शुद्ध करते हैं। जठराग्नि रूप में सबके भीतर बसकर अन्न पचाते हैं। विवाह में साक्षी बनते हैं। अग्निहोत्र, नवग्रह शान्ति हवन — अग्नि देव की कृपा से सब कार्य सिद्ध होते हैं।
benefits: Forty verses honoring Agni Deva — the sacred fire, first deity invoked in the Rig Veda
Enhances the fruit of all havan, yajna, and fire rituals
Purifies body, mind, and environment through the grace of the divine fire
Strengthens digestive fire (Jatharagni) and promotes good health
Auspicious before any fire ceremony — wedding, griha pravesh, navagraha shanti
Connects the devotee to the most ancient Vedic tradition of fire worship
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Agni, Agni Chalisa (अग्नि चालीसा) stands as a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn. This sacred composition is sourced from Vedic Agni Worship Tradition (Rig Veda / Yajur Veda) and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to purify body, mind, and environment through the grace of the divine fire and enhance the fruit of all havan, yajna, and fire rituals. It is also said to forty verses honoring Agni Deva — the sacred fire, first deity invoked in the Rig Veda. This sacred text is ideally recited on Sunday (Ravivar) for enhanced blessings. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 7 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Agni Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Agni Chalisa in Hindi with meaning and transliteration. 40 verses praising Agni Deva, the sacred Vedic fire god.
source: Vedic Agni Worship Tradition (Rig Veda / Yajur Veda)

Sapta jihva seven tongues Agni Vedic
Havan yajna mantras for Agni Devata

Wedding saptapadi seven steps Agni witness

seoFaqs.question: What is the Agni Chalisa and which deity does it praise?
seoFaqs.answer: Agni Chalisa (अग्नि चालीसा) is a forty-verse devotional hymn (chalisa = forty) in Hindi-Sanskrit mishra-bhasha praising Agni Deva — the sacred fire, the first deity invoked in the Rigveda (the very opening hymn 'Agnim-ile purohitam yajnasya devam-ritvijam'), the messenger between humans and devas (deva-dut), and the witness of every dharmic ceremony from birth to death. The composition follows the standard Tulsidasa-style chalisa pattern: opening doha (couplet) of dhyana, forty chaupais (quatrains) of stuti, and closing doha of phalashruti. Each verse celebrates one aspect of Agni — his sapta-jihva (seven flame-tongues), his three ritual forms (Garhapatya, Dakshinagni, Ahavaniya), his consort Svaha, his ascetic name Pavaka, his all-pervasive presence as Vaishvanara (digestive fire), and his role as witness in marriage. (Sources: Vedic Agni Worship Tradition — Rigveda Mandala 1; Yajurveda; chalisa-paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Agni Deva and why is he the first deity of the Rigveda?
seoFaqs.answer: Agni Deva is the personification of fire as a cosmic and ritual deity — pre-eminent in the Vedic pantheon. He is invoked as the very first word of the entire Rigveda: 'Agnim-ile purohitam yajnasya devam-ritvijam, hotaram ratna-dhatamam' — 'I praise Agni, the household priest of yajna, the divine ritvik, the hotar, the supreme bestower of gems'. His primacy is functional: without Agni, no yajna can be performed; without yajna, the devas cannot receive offerings; without offerings, dharma collapses. He is therefore the Purohita (front-placed priest) of the cosmic order. He has three classical forms — Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva are sometimes interpreted as his trinity-aspects in early Vedic theology, with Agni as Vishvarupa. The Brahmanas count him as the mouth of the gods (devanam mukham), the vahaka (carrier) of all havya. (Sources: Rigveda 1.1; Aitareya Brahmana; Shatapatha Brahmana.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the seven tongues of Agni mentioned in this Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 2 of the Agni Chalisa names the sapta-jihva (seven tongues of flame) of Agni from the Mundaka Upanishad (1.2.4): Kali (the dark/blue flame), Karali (the terrifying flame), Manojava (the mind-swift flame), Sulohita (the deep-red flame), Sudhumravarna (the smoke-coloured flame), Sphulingini (the spark-emitting flame), and Vishvarupi (the all-formed flame). Each tongue receives a different category of offering and corresponds to a different psychic-energetic effect on the worshipper. The Mundaka Upanishad warns that one who offers ahuti to the wrong tongue receives the wrong fruit — pointing to the precise science of fire-ritual. The Chalisa simplifies this for the devotee by enumerating the seven names as a single mala of Agni's prabha (radiance). (Sources: Mundaka Upanishad 1.2.4; Vedic ritual tradition; Agni Chalisa verse 2.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the three ritual fires Garhapatya, Dakshinagni and Ahavaniya?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 5 of the Agni Chalisa names the three classical Vedic ritual fires that every srauta (Vedic-yajna householder) must maintain: (1) Garhapatya — the household perpetual fire, lit at marriage and kept alive throughout life, used for daily nityopasana, anvaharya cooking and family rituals; (2) Dakshinagni (or Anvaharya-pacana) — the southern fire, used specifically for offerings to the pitris (ancestors) and certain shanti karmas; (3) Ahavaniya — the eastern offering-fire, into which all major ahutis to the devas are poured, kindled fresh from the Garhapatya for each yajna. These three together form the tretagni — the triad of Vedic fires — which Manu Smriti declares every dvija should maintain. The architecture of the Vedic yajnashala is built around the geometric placement of these three fires. (Sources: Shatapatha Brahmana; Manu Smriti; Yajurveda.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Svaha Devi mentioned as Agni's consort?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 6 names Svaha as Agni's pati-shakti (consort-energy). Svaha is the personification of the sacred utterance 'Svaha!' that must be appended to every offering (ahuti) made to a deva in the fire — without 'Svaha' the offering is technically not 'received'. The Bhagavata Purana (4.1.59) and Brahma Vaivarta Purana narrate her as a daughter of Daksha married to Agni; her body has the divine power of carrying the offered substance to the intended deva — without her, even Agni cannot complete the yajna-circuit. She has six children (the Skanda-shadanga) — the six Krittika-fed forms of Skanda. In daily worship, the offerings 'svaha' (to devas) and 'svadha' (to pitris) are personified as Agni's two queens — Svaha governing dharmic ahuti and Svadha governing pitri-tarpana. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 4.1; Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Vedic ritual tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is Vaishvanara and Jatharagni mentioned in this Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 7 invokes Agni in his Vaishvanara aspect — the universal fire that resides as Jatharagni (digestive fire) in every living being. The Bhagavad Gita 15.14 declares: 'Aham vaishvanaro bhutva praninam deham-ashritah, pranapana-samayuktah pachamy-annam chatur-vidham' — 'Becoming the Vaishvanara fire, established in the bodies of beings, joined with prana and apana, I digest the four kinds of food'. The Chalisa thus internalises Agni — he is not only outside in the yajna-kunda but within every cell as the metabolic fire that turns annam (food) into rasa, blood, flesh, marrow and consciousness. This insight gives Agni Chalisa its unique health-benefit dimension: regular recitation is said to strengthen jathara-agni and cure indigestion, sluggish metabolism and diseases of mandagni. (Sources: Bhagavad Gita 15.14; Chandogya Upanishad; Ayurvedic tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Agni Chalisa be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Agni Chalisa is best recited: (1) Sunday (Ravivar) — sacred to the solar-fire deities, as listed in the source. (2) Before any havan, yajna or fire-ceremony — to invoke Agni's grace for the success of the ritual. (3) On Vasanta Panchami and Holika Dahan — fire-festivals. (4) During Navagraha Shanti homam, Mahamrityunjaya homam, Sudarshana homam — any homam at all. (5) At griha-pravesh and weddings — Agni is the saakshi (witness) of marriage (verse 9 — 'vivaha mein saakshi bano tum, saptapadi agni phere'). (6) Daily by those suffering from chronic indigestion or weak metabolism. (7) On Kartik Purnima — Karthikai Deepam in Tamil tradition is the supreme festival of Agni-jyoti. (8) Before lighting the household kitchen fire for the first time. Recommended count: 1, 3, 7, 11 or 21 times. (Sources: classical chalisa-paddhati; Vedic Agni-pathana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Agni Chalisa bestow?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional phalashruti and the closing doha enumerate: (a) Tan-mana shuddhi — purification of body and mind (Agni is the supreme purifier — pavaka); (b) phalada havan-yajna — all fire-rituals become fruitful when preceded by this Chalisa; (c) digestive health — strengthening of jatharagni, cure of mandagni, anorexia and metabolic disorders (Vaishvanara-aspect); (d) success in shubha-karyas begun with Agni's invocation; (e) protection during fire ceremonies from accidents; (f) merit equivalent to a small homam when full recitation is impossible; (g) for couples — strengthening of the saptapadi vow taken before Agni at marriage; (h) for students — sharpening of intellect (Agni is identified with vak/speech and tejas/brilliance); (i) protection from cold-related diseases and depression (Agni is heat and light); (j) for the sadhaka, awakening of inner tapas-fire and burning of accumulated sanskaras. (Sources: Agni Chalisa phalashruti; classical Vedic Agni-mahatmya.)
